header.title: Hagicode Docker Compose Builder
header.subtitle: Generate Docker Compose configurations for Hagicode

configForm.title: Docker Compose Configuration Generator
configForm.subtitle: Configure your Hagicode Docker Compose setup
configForm.quickStartDescription: Get started quickly with SQLite database, no PostgreSQL configuration needed
configForm.fullCustomDescription: Complete control over all configuration options
configForm.profileHelp: Choose your configuration experience. You can switch modes at any time.
configForm.supportHint: Need help? Join QQ Group {{groupNumber}} or Discord.
configForm.dockerHubNetworkAdvice: Suitable for users with Docker Hub mirror acceleration support
configForm.azureAcrDescription: Alternative image registry, synced with Docker Hub
configForm.azureAcrNetworkAdvice: Suitable for users who cannot access Docker Hub locally
configForm.aliyunAcrDescription: Alibaba Cloud Container Registry, recommended for users in China
configForm.aliyunAcrNetworkAdvice: Suitable for users in Mainland China, providing stable image acceleration services
configForm.enableHttpsHelp: Expose Hagicode through a Caddy reverse proxy with self-signed certificates.
configForm.lanIpHelp: Use your LAN IPv4/IPv6 address for local network access.
configForm.lanIpTooltipText: Use the host machine address reachable by other devices in your local network.
configForm.certificateGuideDescription: Caddy auto-generates self-signed certificates with tls internal.
configForm.certificateStep1: 1. Caddy uses tls internal to create self-signed certificates.
configForm.certificateStep2: 2. Certificates are persisted in Docker volume caddy_data.
configForm.certificateStep3: 3. Trust the certificate in your browser on first visit.
configForm.privilegedPortWarning: Port below 1024 may require elevated privileges.
configForm.localhostLanWarning: localhost is not recommended for LAN HTTPS sharing.
configForm.caddyPreviewTlsHint: `tls internal` is included for self-signed certificate management.
configForm.sqliteDescription: Lightweight embedded database, no additional container needed, ideal for quick start and development environments.
configForm.parallelEnablementHint: Claude, Codex, and OpenCode can run in parallel. They are not mutually exclusive.
configForm.explicitExecutorHint: The generated compose file only exports enabled executor branches. Runtime selection must now be explicit.
configForm.communitySupportTitle: Other CLI tools are supported too
configForm.communitySupportDescription: HagiCode also supports other CLI tools, but the container integration documentation for them is not complete yet.
configForm.communityContributeRelease: Contribute release and runtime contract PRs in https://github.com/HagiCode-org/releases.
configForm.codexDescription: Configure Codex runtime using CODEX_* environment variables
configForm.openCodeDescription: OpenCode uses the managed runtime contract baked into the unified image instead of a default-provider route.
configForm.openCodeModelHint: Optional managed runtime model. The image ships the opencode command even though routing is configured explicitly.
configForm.openCodeTargetPathHint: OpenCode always reads and writes the same in-container opencode.json path.
configForm.openCodeConfigSourceHint: Choose whether Docker Compose should keep the OpenCode config in a managed volume or explicitly bind OpenCode files from the host.
configForm.openCodeConfigModeDefaultHint: Recommended. Uses three named volumes: opencode-config-data, opencode-auth-data, and opencode-models-data, so config, auth, and cache directories survive restarts.
configForm.openCodeConfigModeHostFileHint: Bind a host opencode.json file and optionally add auth.json and models.json mounts.
configForm.openCodeConfigHostPathHint: Enter the absolute path to opencode.json on the Docker host. Example: {{example}}
configForm.openCodeAuthHostPathHint: Optional. To let the container reuse an existing login, enter the absolute path to host auth.json. Example: {{example}}
configForm.openCodeModelsHostPathHint: Optional. To reuse the host model cache, enter the absolute path to models.json. Example: {{example}}
configForm.openCodeConfigBrowserLimitHint: Browsers cannot return a Docker-ready host bind path for you, so this field must be filled manually.
configForm.openCodeConfigManualPriorityHint: The exported compose file always uses the path you type here as the source of truth.
configForm.openCodeAdditionalHostFilesHint: If you fill auth.json or models.json host paths, Builder will export matching bind mounts too. Leave them empty to skip those mounts.
configForm.openCodeManagedVolumeHint: No extra input is required. The builder will automatically mount opencode-config-data, opencode-auth-data, and opencode-models-data for config, auth, and model cache persistence.
configForm.openCodeRuntimePathHint: All OpenCode runtime files inside the container live under the /home/hagicode/ prefix.
configForm.openCodeConfigAuthWarningTitle: Config file is not the auth file
configForm.openCodeConfigAuthWarningHint: opencode.json, auth.json, and models.json are three separate files. Even in host-file mode, each path is configured independently.
configForm.openCodeConfigAuthMigrationHint: If you want to reuse an existing host login, either provide the host auth.json path explicitly or prepare it yourself at /home/hagicode/.local/share/opencode/auth.json. Builder will not copy it automatically.
configForm.codexCompatibilityDescription: You can also use OPENAI_* environment variables as compatibility aliases
configForm.unifiedUseOfToken: Unified use of ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN environment variable
configForm.apiEndpointAutoSet: API Endpoint will be automatically set to
configForm.usingOfficialApi: Using Anthropic official API, no need to set ANTHROPIC_URL
configForm.claudeCodeExtendedConfigDescription: Configure Claude Code model versions and experimental features (optional)
configForm.anthropicSonnetModelHint: ZAI default: glm-4.7 | Anthropic example: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
configForm.anthropicOpusModelHint: ZAI default: glm-5 | Anthropic example: claude-opus-4-20250514
configForm.anthropicHaikuModelHint: ZAI default: glm-4.5-air | Anthropic example: claude-haiku-4-20250514
configForm.claudeCodeExperimentalAgentTeamsHint: Enable experimental Agent Teams collaboration feature
configForm.workdirDescription: Work directory points to a folder containing your code repositories. We will scan multiple repositories within this folder and it will be mapped to /app/workdir inside the container.
configForm.workdirRecommendation: 💡 Recommendation: Point to a parent folder that contains multiple repositories (e.g., /home/user/repos or C:\Users\YourName\source\repos). The application will scan all subfolders to detect repositories.
configForm.workdirCreatedByRoot: Work directory created by root user
configForm.permissionIssueDescription: The work directory was created by root user, which will prevent the container from writing files. Please choose one of the following solutions:
configForm.createNonRootUser: Create a non-root user and use that user to create the directory
configForm.configureUserPermissionDescription: To solve file permission issues inside and outside the container, you need to configure PUID and PGID. Use the user ID and group ID of the user who created the work directory.
configForm.permissionNotRequiredDescription: When workdir is created by root, permission mapping is not required.
configForm.codeServerDescription: Configure the managed browser IDE defaults exported only in full custom mode.
configForm.codeServerEnable: Enable code-server as the default browser IDE
configForm.codeServerEnableHint: When disabled, Builder keeps your saved values but exports code serve-web as the default implementation instead.
configForm.codeServerPrivateHint: No dedicated host port is published until you explicitly enable host publishing below.
configForm.codeServerPersistenceHint: Deployment defaults are exported into compose, while runtime state continues to live under the shared HagiCode data volume after the container starts.
configForm.codeServerHostHint: Use 127.0.0.1 for private container-only access. Switch to 0.0.0.0 or :: before enabling host publishing.
configForm.codeServerPortHint: Valid range: 1-65535. Port 36529 matches the HagiCode code-server default.
configForm.codeServerAuthModeHint: Use password when you want Builder to export a startup secret for code-server.
configForm.codeServerPasswordHint: This value is passed to the container bootstrap path and bridged to code-server without being echoed in comments.
configForm.codeServerPasswordSkippedHint: No password secret is exported while auth mode stays set to none.
configForm.codeServerPublishToHostHint: Adds a dedicated host-to-container port mapping for code-server while keeping the bind address fixed to host loopback.
configForm.codeServerPublishedPortHint: Builder emits a separate loopback-only mapping for this host port.
configForm.codeServerPublishBindHint: Builder keeps the host-side bind fixed to loopback for the first publish step. Add a reverse proxy if you need wider exposure.
configForm.codeServerDisabledHint: Builder will export code serve-web as the active implementation until you re-enable code-server for this profile.
configForm.eulaDescription: Review and confirm the runtime agreement shared across quick start and full custom.
configForm.acceptEula: Accept the container EULA before startup
configForm.acceptEulaHint: Builder exports ACCEPT_EULA=Y only when enabled, and the release entrypoint refuses startup until it sees an accepted opt-in value.
configForm.acceptEulaSharedStateHint: This selection is shared across quick start and full custom.

configPreview.invalidConfigCannotExport: Please fix configuration errors before copy/download.
configPreview.basedOnSettings: This configuration is based on your settings above.
configPreview.previewDescription: Live YAML output stays aligned with the visible workspace sections.
configPreview.exportBlockedTitle: Export is unavailable until the highlighted sections are fixed.
configPreview.exportBlockedDescription: Use the shortcuts below to return to the modules that still have validation issues.
configPreview.exportReadyTitle: This configuration is ready to export.
configPreview.exportReadyDescription: Copy or download the generated file whenever you are satisfied with the preview.

backToTop.ariaLabel: Scroll back to top of page

workspace.headerSubtitle: Guide the setup with visible sections, status tracking, and a persistent preview rail.
workspace.introTitle: Build docker-compose.yml with a clearer workspace
workspace.introDescription: Work through grouped deployment sections, keep validation status visible, and open the YAML preview without losing your place.
workspace.navigationDescription: Jump between visible modules and keep hidden branches out of the path.
workspace.previewShortcutDescription: Use the preview rail or drawer to verify output while editing.
workspace.mobilePreviewDescription: Inspect the generated docker-compose.yml without leaving the current section.
workspace.repositoryHint: Repository mapping stays in this section
workspace.repositoryHintDescription: Keep the workspace mount close to permission settings so Linux deployments stay aligned.
workspace.licenseDescription: Choose the shared public key or provide a custom license for this deployment.
workspace.inlineErrorsTitle: {{count}} issue needs attention in this section

metrics.completedDetail: Modules that are ready to export
metrics.exportBlockedDetail: Preview is live, export stays disabled

profile.title: Deployment mode
profile.description: Choose quick start or full custom before editing deeper options.

base.title: Base deployment
base.description: Configure ports, image source, and baseline runtime details.

executors.title: Executor capabilities
executors.description: Enable runtime branches and fill the credentials each active branch needs.

https.title: HTTPS access
https.description: Expose the deployment through Caddy only when HTTPS is enabled.

database.title: Database strategy
database.description: Pick SQLite, an internal PostgreSQL service, or an external database.

advanced.title: Advanced setup
advanced.description: Handle workspace mounts, permissions, license settings, and optional Claude tuning.

claude.title: Claude credentials
claude.description: Anthropic-compatible provider, endpoint, and token settings.

opencode.title: OpenCode runtime
opencode.description: Managed model selection plus config persistence mode.

codeServer.title: Code Server defaults
codeServer.description: Container host/port, auth mode, and optional host publishing for code-server.

eula.title: Container EULA
eula.description: Shared ACCEPT_EULA opt-in plus the docs agreement link.
